skill.name: wechat-article-search-api-skill
skill.description: Extract full article contents from WeChat using the BrowserAct API. Search by keywords and date filters to retrieve structured article data including title, author, publication date, image URL, and full body text.
skill.summary: Search and extract structured WeChat articles via the BrowserAct API using keywords and date filters.
skill.author: browser-act

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer flagged many patterns, but evaluation shows the vast majority are false positives. The Python script makes legitimate HTTPS requests to the documented BrowserAct API and reads a single environment variable (BROWSERACT_API_KEY) for authentication. The SKILL.md backtick findings are documentation examples, not executable commands. No credential exfiltration, code execution, or malicious behavior was found. Risk is low due to expected network and env_access behavior for an API-integrated skill.

low_findings.title: Network requests to third-party API
low_findings.description: The script makes HTTPS POST and GET requests to api.browseract.com. This is expected behavior for an API-integrated skill and is documented in SKILL.md. No exfiltration or unauthorized endpoints are contacted.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network calls are documented, legitimate API interactions with the declared BrowserAct service. No suspicious domains or data leakage patterns are present.

low_findings.title: Environment variable access for API key
low_findings.description: The script reads BROWSERACT_API_KEY from the environment. This is a standard and safe pattern for supplying user-provided credentials to a third-party API. The key is used only in the Authorization header for requests to the documented API.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Reading a single documented environment variable for API authentication is a normal, low-risk pattern with no evidence of credential harvesting.

content.value_statement: Finding full WeChat articles manually is slow and fragmented. This skill uses the BrowserAct API to search by keywords and return structured article data including full body text, author, and publication date.
actual_capabilities: Search WeChat articles by keywords with optional date filters
Retrieve structured data including title, author, publication date, image URL, and full body content
Poll long-running browser automation tasks and report status
Handle missing API keys gracefully with user guidance
Retry once on non-authentication failures automatically
limitations: Requires a valid BrowserAct API key configured in the environment
Results depend on BrowserAct service availability and task completion time
Publication date filtering may require Chinese date formats for best results
Maximum polling time is 5 minutes per task

use_cases.title: Brand mention monitoring
use_cases.description: Track mentions of a brand or product across WeChat articles to measure public relations exposure and sentiment.

use_cases.title: Competitor content analysis
use_cases.description: Collect and review full-length posts from competitor WeChat public accounts to analyze messaging and positioning.

use_cases.title: Industry trend research
use_cases.description: Gather articles on emerging topics such as AI agents or automation to build an internal knowledge base.

prompt_templates.title: Basic keyword search
prompt_templates.prompt: Search WeChat articles for the keyword 'openclaw' and return the titles and authors.

prompt_templates.title: Filtered search with date
prompt_templates.prompt: Find WeChat articles about 'AI agent' published on March 11 and extract the full body content for each.

prompt_templates.title: Batch research with limits
prompt_templates.prompt: Search WeChat for 'browser automation' with a limit of 20 articles. Provide a summary table with title, author, publication date, and URL.

prompt_templates.title: Competitor monitoring workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Monitor WeChat articles mentioning 'CompetitorX' from the last week. Extract full text and flag any pricing or product announcements.

output_examples.output:
Title: OpenClaw Introduction
Author: TechDaily
Publication Date: 2026-03-11
URL: https://mp.weixin.qq.com/s/example
Image URL: https://mmbiz.qpic.cn/example.jpg
Body: OpenClaw is a new automation framework...

output_examples.input: Find articles about 'AI agent' published on March 11

best_practices: Provide the BrowserAct API key before running searches to avoid interruptions
Start with a small limit such as 5 to 10 articles to verify results before larger batches
Use specific keywords rather than broad terms to improve result relevance
anti_patterns: Do not share your BrowserAct API key in public chats or logs
Do not assume English date formats will work; use Chinese formats when possible
Do not retry repeatedly on 'Invalid authorization' errors; update the API key instead

faq.question: Why do I need a BrowserAct API key?
faq.answer: The skill relies on the BrowserAct API to run browser automation tasks that extract WeChat article data. The API key authenticates your requests to that service.

faq.question: How do I get a BrowserAct API key?
faq.answer: Visit the BrowserAct Console at https://www.browseract.com/reception/integrations, sign in, and copy your API key. Then provide it when the skill asks for it.

faq.question: How long does a search take?
faq.answer: Searches can take several minutes because the API performs real browser automation. The script prints timestamped status updates so you can monitor progress.

faq.question: What date formats work best?
faq.answer: Chinese formats such as '3月11日' are recommended. You can also try 'March 10' or '2026-03-11', but results may vary depending on the article metadata.

faq.question: What happens if the task fails?
faq.answer: If the failure is not due to an invalid API key, the skill will retry once automatically. If it fails again, the error message is reported so you can investigate.

faq.question: Can I use this skill with Claude, Codex, or Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill is compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. The agent will run the script and interpret the results for you.
